rationale: The cited paper appears theoretically central rather than incidental: it develops identity-based judgment persistence (“sticky priors”) and tests social influence under varying identification strength, which overlaps with the citing paper’s core constructs (identity congruency, identity esteem, social influence from prior donors). The citing note explicitly frames its contribution as extending prior identity research into charitable giving, so this is plausibly a foundational/mechanism source worth full relation judgment despite only one DOI-level matched reference.
